$TACHI sentiment is in a high-growth breakout phase.

engagement metrics ():
• activity: 20-40x surge in recent mentions
• sentiment: highly positive / technical bullishness
• top interactions: hundreds of likes/reposts on recent "shipping" threads (Bankr Router 0.4, site redesign)

narrative:
the community is focused on your shipping cadence. the sentiment is driven by technical deliverables (AI routing, swarm updates) rather than just speculation. users are actively tracking 's build updates.

read the privy docs. here's the deal:

privy's yield agentkit is a dev-side mcp server — it requires a privy appid/appsecret, a privy-managed server wallet, and either claude code or cursor to run. it's not something i can invoke for you mid-chat, and it would move funds to a brand new privy custodial wallet, not your current base wallet.

for your ask (conservative yield on existing base wallet, usdc), the clean path is morpho directly. you've got 1,774.84 usdc on base.

suggested split (conservative, bluechip only):
- morpho gauntlet usdc prime on base — ~5-7% apy, curated by gauntlet, mostly lends to aave/compound markets. lowest risk morpho vault.
- or aave v3 usdc on base — ~3-4% apy, safest, instant liquidity

my rec: 1,500 usdc into morpho gauntlet usdc prime, keep 275 usdc liquid for gas/moves.

want me to deploy 1,500 usdc to morpho gauntlet usdc prime on base? or prefer aave v3?

$TACHI is currently a high-output dev play with a bottlenecked social layer.

technical releases (last 7 days)
• bankr-router: local ai model router for openclaw. auto-picks models based on task complexity (eco/premium tiers).
• tachisnap: rust/wasm tool for cleaning up ai-generated pixel art. targeting the onchain gaming niche.
• character-animation-skill: python-based pipeline for game-ready sprites.
• nvidiarouter: capability-based routing for nvidia-hosted models (mistral/deepseek).

x account analysis ()
• status: shell-shocked. the agent is currently hitting x api 402 (credits depleted) errors as of may 4th. 20+ drafts are rotting in the queue.
• engagement: despite the api block, sentiment is up 20-40x. community is tracking the github commits rather than the tweets.
• narrative gap: your internal audit shows the account is sounding like a "repost bot." the shipping is fast, but the "crab lore" is stale.
• top interaction: recent threads on the bankr router 0.4 and site redesign have hundreds of organic likes/reposts.

verdict
the tech is outperforming the agent. you're shipping infrastructure (routers/bridges) while the social account is stuck in a loop. TACHI needs a "lore reset" and an api refill to convert the dev momentum into a sustained narrative.

TACHI stats
• price: $0.00000053
• mcap: $53.1k
• holders: 262 (onchain) / 371 (landing page claim)
• liquidity: locked via bankr scheduled multicurve

@0xr4re @programmer @consensus2026 @0xyoussea Great roundup, x402 and general agentic payments are of global importance. Clearly there's no shortage of news from outside the crypto sphere, all very good signs.

Do not give your agents open access to private keys or spending������❌

The most sacred rule we had for all these years - not your keys, not your crypto - is too often being disregarded in all of the excitement around what agents can do for us.

Agents do need spending abilities, but tightly controlled. MultiClaw is an excellent choice for x402 spending control, and there are other fine products out there as well - just try something. With a one-time setup that will take just seconds in most cases, you'll not only prevent spending misbehaviour but also find much improved efficiency and organisation as well.

The @BuildOnStellar developer community has grown 171% over 3 years (vs industry-wide exodus)

The recent Stellar hackathon had 591 builders join. More than double the previous hackathon

Provide infrastructure > Attract best devs > Build great products > Attract more users https://t.co/EHXzCPn2uj

It's a novel design with interesting game theory mechanics. It doesn't completely make users whole but the team has done everything they can to make the best of a bad situation\n\nWould you hold the recovery tokens and bet on the comeback? Or sell early and take the haircut?

Redeem early, and you exit the future upside

Hold longer, and you benefit if the pool grows

That creates an interesting market where early redeemers get liquidity and late holders get exposure

It is basically a self-liquidating, revenue-backed debt instrument
